1 / faceDetector module A module to detect faces, using OpenCV. The browser version uses the UC Irvine and Intel OpenCV.js port, https://github.com/ucisysarch/opencvjs . To run an example, please point your browser to: https://ptolemy.berkeley.edu/a

nt your browser to: https://ptolemy.berkeley.edu/accessors/hosts/browser/demo/faceDetector/faceDetector.html Other image processing transformations are available in the computer vision module. To try, please see: https://ptolemy.berkeley.edu/accesso

, please see: https://ptolemy.berkeley.edu/accessors/hosts/browser/demo/computerVision/computerVision.html Functions The following functions are implemented by this module: Filter() : Construct a new object to invoke the face detection filter. filter

object to invoke the face detection filter. filter(image, options, callback) : Detect faces with the given options and call callback() with the result image with any faces marked by squares. image is a path to the image (browser) or the image itself

a path to the image (browser) or the image itself (Cape Code). options are options.maxFaceSize and options.minFaceSize . numberofFaces() : Returns the number of faces detected. Filter uses the event emitter pattern common in JavaScript, emitting: rea

mitter pattern common in JavaScript, emitting: ready : Emitted when an image has successfully loaded from the specified path in setInput(input) . Usage Import the module: var faceDetector = require('faceDetector'); Invoke a filter and handle the resu

aceDetector'); Invoke a filter and handle the result. For example, in an accessor with an input image and outputs result and faceCount : var self = this; this.addInputHandler('input', function() { var image = this.get('input'); v

{ var image = this.get('input'); var options = {}; options.minFaceSize = 50; options.maxFaceSize = 400; faceDetector.filter(image, 'findEdges', options, function(result) { self.send('output', result);
on(result) { self.send('output', result); var numFaces = faceDetector.numberOfFaces(); self.send('faceCount', numFaces); }); }); Browser display The browser version uses the ImageProcessingDisplay

e browser version uses the ImageProcessingDisplay module to automatically set up before and after canvases. See Also Accessor demo OpenCV API Javascript OpenCV.js Back to Optional JavaScript Modules Edit - History - Print - Recent Changes - Search Pa
